title:
Implement naive Fp

prime field with FiniteField + ConstField
description:
Implement a minimal const-generic prime field type Fp<const P: u64> using simple % reduction. This is the second concrete implementation of the FiniteField trait (after Gf2mElement) and the first for characteristic != 2.

Implements both FiniteField and ConstField traits. Validates that:
- The trait generalizes beyond GF(2^m)
- Sub and Neg work correctly for odd characteristic
- zero() / one() work as freestanding associated functions via ConstField
- The axiom test harness works for prime fields

The naive implementation is intentionally unoptimized — Montgomery multiplication (8ce6f8aa) replaces the internals later without changing the public API.

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Debug)]
pub struct Fp<const P: u64>(u64);
```

Inverse via Fermat's little theorem: a^(P-2) mod P.
Add: (a + b) % P with branchless correction.
Sub: (a + P - b) % P.
Mul: ((a as u128) * (b as u128) % (P as u128)) as u64.

Must pass the generic field axiom test harness for at least Fp<2>, Fp<3>, Fp<5>, Fp<7>, Fp<65537>, Fp<(2^61 - 1)>.

Location: crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/mod.rs (new module)
